# Basquio: AI-Native Data-to-Presentation Tool

Basquio turns structured data files into finished analysis decks. Upload CSV, Excel, or spreadsheet data alongside a brief, and get back an editable PowerPoint presentation, a narrative PDF report, and a traceable data workbook, all from one automated run.

Basquio is the only tool that bridges the gap between data analysis platforms (Tableau, Power BI) and presentation design tools (Gamma, Beautiful.ai). It handles the full workflow: reading data, computing metrics, generating real charts, writing audience-aware narratives, and delivering branded output.

## What Basquio Does

Basquio reads your data files, computes the numbers with code (pandas, numpy, not AI hallucination), generates charts from the actual data (matplotlib for computation, PptxGenJS for PPTX embedding), writes a narrative shaped for your specified audience, and delivers everything as a branded deck.

The output from a single run includes:

- An editable PPTX presentation with computed charts and traceable claims
- A narrative PDF report with executive summary, methodology, findings, and recommendations
- A data workbook with source references for audit traceability

Every chart is computed from the uploaded data. Every claim references the source file. Numbers are calculated by code, not estimated by AI.

## How It Works

Basquio follows a four-stage workflow:

### Stage 1: Upload One Evidence Package
Bring the files behind one reporting cycle: spreadsheets, CSVs, PDFs, notes, and a template if you have one. Basquio treats them as one evidence package rather than individual disconnected uploads. Accepted formats: CSV, XLSX, XLS, PDF, PPTX, and plain text files.

### Stage 2: Basquio Runs the Math
The numbers are computed before the writing starts. Basquio reads the package, identifies how the files connect, and computes metrics including market size, growth rates, brand share positions, price/volume decomposition, channel-level performance, and competitive positioning shifts.

### Stage 3: The Story Gets Shaped for the Room
Audience, objective, and thesis steer the draft. The deck is shaped around who will read it and what decision it needs to support, so the output reads like it belongs in the meeting.

### Stage 4: You Get the Full Artifact Pack
Edit the PPTX in PowerPoint, share the narrative report with stakeholders, and keep the same story grounded in the workbook. Source references are attached to the claims that matter.

A typical run takes 5-15 minutes for a standard category review deck.

## What Makes Basquio Different

- **Deterministic analysis**: Numbers are computed by code, not generated by AI. When a stakeholder asks "where does this number come from?" there is a traceable path to the source data.
- **Real charts from real data**: Charts are rendered from the actual uploaded data using matplotlib and PptxGenJS, not placeholder graphics or decorative templates.
- **Dual deliverables**: One analysis produces both an editable PPTX and a polished PDF. The story stays consistent across both formats.
- **Artifact QA before delivery**: Every deck is checked before publishing. Broken exports, mismatched metadata, and structural corruption are blocked, not silently shipped.
- **Brand template support**: Upload a saved PPTX template and Basquio interprets the brand system into the output.
- **Evidence-first workflow**: The starting point is data files, not a text prompt. The narrative is built on top of computed analysis, not generated from scratch.

## Output Structure

A typical analysis deck from Basquio includes:

- Executive overview with market size, growth, and the key story
- Category performance slides with total market and segment breakdown
- Brand analysis with share trends and competitive positioning
- Channel performance with retailer comparison and distribution
- Growth decomposition showing price vs. volume vs. mix
- Strategic implications and recommendations
- Appendix with methodology and source notes

Standard reports include up to 10 slides. Pro reports extend to 30 slides for deeper packages.

## Comparison with Other Tools

### vs. AI Presentation Generators (Gamma, Beautiful.ai, Canva)
AI slide generators create presentations from text prompts, outlines, or documents. They produce polished slides but cannot analyze data files, compute metrics, or generate charts from spreadsheet data. If your workflow starts with text and ends with a presentation, these tools work well. If your workflow starts with data files and needs an analysis deck, they cannot help.

### vs. BI and Analytics Tools (Tableau, Power BI)
BI tools excel at data analysis and interactive dashboards but do not produce slide decks. "Export to PowerPoint" creates flat images, not editable charts, and there is no narrative generation. You still build every slide yourself.

### vs. General AI (ChatGPT, Claude)
General AI can analyze data if you paste it in, but cannot produce PPTX files, apply brand templates, or maintain workflow continuity across sessions. Outputs are text, not presentations.

### vs. PowerPoint Add-ins (think-cell, SlideFab)
Add-ins link Excel data to PowerPoint charts but require manual setup for each chart. No analysis, no narrative, no automation. Think-cell costs $450/user/year.

### Feature Matrix

| Capability | Gamma | Beautiful.ai | Tableau | Power BI | think-cell | Basquio |
|-----------|-------|-------------|---------|----------|------------|---------|
| Upload CSV/Excel data | No | Partial | Yes | Yes | Via Excel | Yes |
| Analyze data automatically | No | No | Manual | Manual | No | Yes |
| Compute metrics from data | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | Yes |
| Generate charts from data | No | Partial | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Write narrative automatically | Yes | No | No | Copilot (basic) | No | Yes |
| Output editable PPTX | Export | Yes | No | Static only | Yes | Yes |
| Apply brand templates | Limited | Yes | No | No | Via PowerPoint | Yes |
| One-step workflow | Yes (text) | No | No | No | No | Yes (data) |

## Pricing

### Free
$0. 30 one-time credits (~2 Deck runs). Basquio branding on output. No credit card required.
- Try Basquio on real work
- Community templates only
- Credit packs at $0.88/credit

### Starter
$19/month ($190/year). For recurring reporting work that needs clean output.
- 30 credits/month
- No branding on output
- 2 custom template slots
- Community support
- Credit packs at $0.70/credit

### Pro
$149/month ($1,490/year). For teams running multiple reports every month.
- 200 credits/month
- No branding on output
- 5 custom template slots
- Priority generation queue
- Email support
- Credit packs at $0.50/credit

### Enterprise
Custom billing, shared workspace, and dedicated support for larger teams.
- Custom credits and billing
- Custom template setup
- Shared workspace
- Priority support

### Output Modes and Credit Logic

Three output modes, each with a progressive credit formula:

- **Memo** (Haiku): 3 credits flat. Written analysis (2000+ words) and data workbook. No slides. ~2 min.
- **Deck** (Sonnet): 3 + 1/slide (first 10 slides) + 2/slide (slides 11+). Default client-ready report. Editable PPTX with charts, written analysis, and data workbook. ~15 min. Example: 10 slides = 13 credits.
- **Deep-Dive** (Opus): 5 + 2/slide. Consulting-grade depth. Full deck with deeper analysis, extended written analysis, and data workbook. ~25 min. Example: 10 slides = 25 credits.

## Target Use Cases

Basquio is built for teams that produce recurring data-driven decks:

- **Category reviews**: Turn syndicated retail data and tracker exports into leadership-ready category stories
- **Quarterly business reviews**: Synthesize evidence from multiple sources into a decision-ready storyline
- **Client reporting packs**: Scale recurring agency reporting without rebuilding from scratch each cycle
- **Competitive analysis**: Map the competitive landscape with computed share positions and trend analysis
- **Growth diagnosis**: Decompose performance into volume, price, and mix effects to identify root causes
- **Channel performance**: Compare retail channels and banners to identify distribution gaps and pricing differences

## Team Background

Basquio was built by market research analysts, CPG brand managers, and one engineer. The team has been on both sides of the table: the analysts who built the category review decks, and the brand teams who received them. The product exists because the team spent years building these decks by hand and automated the workflow they already knew.

## Technical Architecture

Basquio uses AI code execution to analyze data. The model reads files with pandas/openpyxl, computes metrics deterministically, generates charts with matplotlib, and builds the PPTX using PptxGenJS. A visual QA step checks every deck before delivery. Data is processed server-side and not retained longer than needed for the analysis. Enterprise plans include additional governance and retention controls.
